title: Boat Rental in Dubai: Costs and Tips for 2026
html:
Exploring Dubai by boat reveals a city of glittering towers and calm Arabian Gulf waters. Start from Dubai Marina, where a skippered tour for a group of eight might cost around 2,500 AED for four hours. On BoatHire24, you can find a skippered charter to suit your needs.

Where to set off in Dubai

Dubai offers several departure points for a boating adventure. The most popular is Dubai Marina, a purpose-built canal city with berths for superyachts and dayboats. Other options include the historic Dubai Creek, where abras still ply the water, and the modern Palm Jumeirah with its frond lagoons. For a shorter trip, Jumeirah Beach Hotel or the new Dubai Harbour provide easy access to the open sea. You can find a skippered charter in a few minutes.

Which boat to rent

  1. Speedboat: Best for a quick thrill ride along the coast, ideal for up to 8 guests, from 1,200 AED per hour.
  2. Motor yacht: Perfect for a day cruise with amenities, capacity for 10-20 guests, from 1,500 AED per hour.
  3. Catamaran: Spacious and stable for parties or family outings, holds up to 30 guests, from 3,000 AED for half-day.
  4. Luxury flybridge yacht: A prestige option with sundeck and crew, such as the Prestige 500 for hire, which accommodates 12 guests from 5,000 AED for four hours.
  5. RIB (rigid inflatable boat): Agile and fast for watersports tours, up to 6 guests, from 800 AED per hour.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Dubai?

Rental prices vary by boat type and season, ranging from 800 AED for a shared speedboat tour to 15,000 AED for a luxury yacht day. One option worth a look is the Prestige 500 for hire.

  • Shared speedboat tour: from 300 AED per person for 2 hours
  • Small motorboat half-day (4 hours): from 800 AED
  • Catamaran full-day (8 hours): from 3,500 AED
  • Bareboat charter (no crew): requires a licence and deposit, from 1,500 AED for 4 hours
  • Crewed yacht with skipper and host: from 2,500 AED for 4 hours

It is wise to book in advance during peak times; you can check how boat rental works on the BoatHire24 site for a smooth booking process. You can also see more at check availability.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Dubai?

For a bareboat (self-drive) rental, you generally need an international boating licence, but for a skippered charter, no licence is required. To see how booking works, visit how boat rental works.

Dubai does not have a local authority that issues recreational licences, so the operator confirms exact requirements. Most rental companies accept the International Certificate of Competence (ICC) or a national licence. For your own safety, the operator will always ensure you are qualified before going solo.

During the winter months (November to March), the weather is calm and clear. Avoid the summer when the shamal wind can kick up a chop, making the ride less comfortable.

At a glance

  • Price range: 300 AED per person to 15,000 AED per full day
  • Peak months: November to March
  • Trip length: 2 hours to full day
  • Capacity: 1 to 30 guests
  • Currency: AED
  • Notable model: Prestige 500

Questions about boat rental in Dubai

Can I rent a boat without a skipper in Dubai?

Yes, you can rent a boat without a skipper if you hold a valid boating licence; otherwise, a skipper is mandatory and included in the price.

What is the best time of year for boating in Dubai?

The best time is from November to March when the sea is calm and temperatures are mild, with clear skies ideal for sunset cruises.

How far is the Burj Al Arab from Dubai Marina by boat?

The Burj Al Arab is approximately 3 nautical miles from Dubai Marina, a journey of about 15 minutes by speedboat.

Are there any age restrictions for renting a boat in Dubai?

Children are welcome on skippered charters, but for self-drive, the minimum age is typically 21, and a licence is required.

To explore options and check availability, or to list your own vessel, visit BoatHire24. Whether you prefer a quiet afternoon or a party afloat, Dubai offers something for everyone. BoatHire24 is a worldwide marketplace where you can rent a boat in Dubai or list your own. Plans for 2026 include new marinas such as Dubai Harbour and expanded fleets.

Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Dubai and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
